title: alstonia actinophylla
intro: Alstonia actinophylla, also known as the Queensland arrowroot or strychnine tree, is a plant native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alstonia actinophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots and leaves, which can be dried, powdered, or used in teas and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
